body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Use mission_id when deciding approvals**

When a user clicks approve/reject, this passes the approval id into `approveMission`, but the API client maps that argument to `POST /api/missions/{id}/approve`. The backend route loads a mission by that path parameter, so any pending approval whose id differs from its mission id returns `mission_not_found`/400 and the dashboard cannot approve or reject requests; use the selected approval's `mission_id` for the route, including the reject path.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Verify receipt evidence through EvidenceStore**

This receipt-chain verifier declares a receipt verifiable by recomputing only the content hash, so a stored evidence payload with a matching `sha256` but a broken evidence envelope (for example wrong `evidence_id`, wrong `mission_id`, invalid `envelope_sha256`, or invalid origin transition) can still make `chain_intact` true for `/api/receipts` and the independent auditor. Call the public `EvidenceStore.verify()`/integrity-bound path for each `receipt_id` before trusting it instead of duplicating only part of verification.

AGENTS.md reference: [AGENTS.md:L63-L63](https://github.com/Zsx154855/NEXARA-PRIME/blob/f220c3d662a01cb12c02db8376f2b713069511f1/AGENTS.md#L63-L63)

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Verify receipt evidence through EvidenceStore**

This receipt-chain verifier declares a receipt verifiable by recomputing only the content hash, so a stored evidence payload with a matching `sha256` but a broken evidence envelope (for example wrong `evidence_id`, wrong `mission_id`, invalid `envelope_sha256`, or invalid origin transition) can still make `chain_intact` true for `/api/receipts` and the independent auditor. Call the public `EvidenceStore.verify()`/integrity-bound path for each `receipt_id` before trusting it instead of duplicating only part of verification.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Put repository excerpts in the model prompt**

For source-dir missions using a real OpenAI-compatible provider, the full repository context (`files` and `excerpts`) is placed only in `metadata`, while the chat `messages` still contain just `system` and the compiled task (which includes only a 500-character context summary from `TokenCompiler`). Chat metadata is not prompt content, so most real repository file excerpts never reach the model even though later stages report a context-bound provider result; include the bounded excerpts in a user/developer message or another model-visible input.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Fetch real tool invocations for mission details**

The mission workspace calls `api.fetchTools(missionId)` to populate the Tool Invocations and failure-code panels, but this method is a hard-coded empty result; a repo-wide search for `/api/tools`, `fetchTools`, and `list_invocations` shows no backend endpoint wired to replace it. Any completed or failed tool calls for the selected mission are therefore always hidden in the UI, including the failure codes added by this change.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

@@ -72,15 +72,74 @@ def __init__(self, store: SQLiteStore, events: EventBus):
     def write(self, kind: MemoryKind, key: str, content: str, trace_id: str, mission_id: str | None = None, source_evidence_id: str | None = None, confidence: float = 1.0) -> MemoryRecord:
         if kind == MemoryKind.UNVERIFIED_INFERENCE:
             return self.propose(kind, key, content, trace_id, mission_id, source_evidence_id, confidence)
+        # Evidence-binding enforcement: mission-scoped committed memories SHOULD have
+        # evidence linkage per NSEC 第四十三条. DECISION/FAILURE/FAILURE_EXPERIENCE/PATCH
+        # require evidence when tied to a mission. System-level kinds and working memory
+        # are exempt.
+        _EVIDENCE_REQUIRED_KINDS = {
+            MemoryKind.DECISION, MemoryKind.FAILURE,
+            MemoryKind.FAILURE_EXPERIENCE, MemoryKind.PATCH,
+        }
+        evidence_bound = bool(source_evidence_id)
+        if not evidence_bound and kind in _EVIDENCE_REQUIRED_KINDS and mission_id:
+            raise ValueError(
+                f"memory_requires_evidence: kind={kind.value} requires "
+                f"source_evidence_id when bound to a mission"
+            )
         record = MemoryRecord(
             memory_id=new_id("memory"), mission_id=mission_id, kind=kind, key=key,
             content=content, source_evidence_id=source_evidence_id, confidence=confidence,
-            status="committed", verified=bool(source_evidence_id), canonical=bool(source_evidence_id),
+            status="committed", verified=evidence_bound, canonical=evidence_bound,
         )
         self.store.save_record(record.memory_id, "memory", record.model_dump(mode="json"), record.created_at, mission_id)
-        self.events.publish("memory.written", mission_id or "global", "mission" if mission_id else "memory", "memory_kernel", trace_id, {"memory_id": record.memory_id, "kind": kind.value})
+        self.events.publish("memory.written", mission_id or "global", "mission" if mission_id else "memory", "memory_kernel", trace_id, {"memory_id": record.memory_id, "kind": kind.value, "evidence_bound": evidence_bound})
         return record
 
+    def verify_evidence_binding(self, mission_id: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
+        """Verify that all committed memories have valid evidence bindings.
+
+        Returns a structured report of violations and integrity status.
+        """
+        records = self.inspect(mission_id)
+        violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+        bound = 0
+        unbound = 0
+        exempt = 0
+
+        for r in records:
+            if r.get("status") != "committed":
+                continue
+            kind_str = r.get("kind", "")
+            try:
+                kind = MemoryKind(kind_str)
+            except ValueError:
+                kind = MemoryKind.FACT
+
+            has_evidence = bool(r.get("source_evidence_id"))
+            is_exempt = kind in {MemoryKind.SHORT_TERM, MemoryKind.TEMPORARY_CONTEXT, MemoryKind.UNVERIFIED_INFERENCE}
+
+            if has_evidence:
+                bound += 1
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Validate memory evidence bindings by record integrity**

This treats any non-empty `source_evidence_id` as a valid binding, so a committed mission memory can point at a nonexistent or corrupt evidence id and still make `verify_evidence_binding()` return `all_bound: true`; that result is then consumed by the independent auditor as proof of memory integrity. Resolve the evidence id through the store/EvidenceStore and require it to be verified for the same mission before incrementing `bound`.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep real context collection inside approved roots**

When `source_dir` is provided, planning now calls the new repository collector even after the workspace-scoped `file_read` path rejected or skipped an outside directory. In a real-provider deployment, any API caller who can create a mission can point `source_dir` at any readable Git root on the server and have up to 24 text excerpts included in the provider request, bypassing the prior approved-workspace boundary; restrict this collector to an approved root or require explicit approval before collecting/sending repository contents.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Serve the built dashboard, not legacy static files**

On a fresh checkout there is no `ui/out/index.html` in the repo, so this fallback mounts `ui/` and serves the old `ui/index.html` rather than the new Next dashboard source added in this commit. Unless an external build step happens before the FastAPI app starts, `/console` never shows the new React UI; make the runtime fail/skip when the export is missing or wire the build artifact into deployment instead of silently falling back to legacy static files.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

diff_hunk:
@@ -431,5 +437,59 @@ def _audit(self, event_type: str, mission_id: str, task_id: str, action: str, de
             metadata=metadata,
         )
 
+    @staticmethod
+    def _classify_failure(exc: Exception) -> tuple[FailureCode, ReasonCode]:
+        """Map every exception type to deterministic FailureCode + ReasonCode.
+
+        This is the SINGLE classification point for all tool failures.
+        Verifier/Auditor/Recovery depend on these codes being stable.
+        """
+        exc_msg = str(exc)
+
+        if isinstance(exc, KeyError) and exc_msg.startswith("unknown_tool:"):
+            return FailureCode.TOOL_UNKNOWN, ReasonCode.TOOL_NOT_REGISTERED
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Classify pre-dispatch tool failures**

This new `TOOL_UNKNOWN` mapping does not classify the normal unknown-tool path: `invoke()` raises `KeyError` before entering the `try` block that persists failed invocations, and even direct classification of `KeyError("unknown_tool:x")` misses because `str(KeyError(...))` includes quotes. Unknown tools therefore still produce no receipt and no deterministic failure code despite the new failure-code/reporting contract; classify and persist these pre-dispatch failures before re-raising.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep provider metadata flat for real context calls**

When a repository context is present, this sends the full redacted context object as Chat Completions `metadata`, including nested `repository`, `files`, and `excerpts`. OpenAI documents metadata as a small set of string key/value pairs, so real OpenAI-compatible providers that enforce that schema will reject these context-bound calls before the model request succeeds; keep only flat strings such as `nexara_context_hash` in metadata and leave the full context in the message payload. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/resources/chat/subresources/completions/methods/update/

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Match memory verification to required evidence kinds**

`write()` only requires mission-scoped evidence for `DECISION`, `FAILURE`, `FAILURE_EXPERIENCE`, and `PATCH`, but this verifier treats every committed non-working memory kind as a violation when it lacks evidence. A legal mission-scoped `FACT` committed via `write()` therefore makes `all_bound` false and can cause the independent auditor to reject repository-context missions even though the write path allowed it; reuse the same evidence-required kind set here.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

diff_hunk:
@@ -138,6 +138,21 @@ def is_allowed(line: str) -> bool:
     return any(re.search(pattern, line, re.IGNORECASE) for pattern in ALLOWED_PATTERNS)
 
 
+def is_self_referential(match: re.Match) -> bool:
+    """Return True when the matched value equals the key name (enum self-reference)."""
+    key_name = match.group(1)
+    matched_text = match.group(0).strip().rstrip(",")
+    variants = {
+        f'{key_name} = "{key_name}"',
+        f"{key_name} = '{key_name}'",
+        f'{key_name}: "{key_name}"',
+        f"{key_name}: '{key_name}'",
+        f'"{key_name}": "{key_name}"',
+        f"'{key_name}': '{key_name}'",
+    }
+    return matched_text in variants
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep weak literal passwords in secret-scan findings**

This exemption also suppresses real weak credentials whose literal value equals the key, such as `password = "password"` or `token: "token"`. Those are hardcoded secrets the scanner is supposed to fail closed on, so this new shortcut lets common credential placeholders through without any finding; restrict the exception to actual enum/constants instead of all secret-bearing names.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Execute approved missions from the dashboard**

After this approval call the backend only advances the mission to `Execution`; it does not run it, and I checked the UI screens for `runMission` and found it is defined only in the API client, never invoked by any component. A mission created and approved entirely in the dashboard therefore remains unexecuted unless the user leaves the dashboard for CLI/API, so add an explicit run action for `Execution` missions or intentionally trigger `/run` after approval.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Populate the plan panel from the saved mission plan**

The backend creates `mission.plan.steps` during `/plan`, but the workspace unconditionally replaces the plan display with an empty array instead of reading those steps or calling a plan endpoint. As a result, every planned mission shows “暂无规划步骤” even after planning succeeded, hiding the execution plan from the dashboard.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Verify tool envelopes before passing receipt chains**

This receipt-chain check starts from raw `list_records` payloads; `SQLiteStore.list_records` returns decoded JSON without validating the stored record envelope. If a tool row is tampered but still points at a valid receipt, the report can still mark the chain intact even though the tool record itself is corrupt, so enumerate through envelope/audit APIs before calculating `chain_intact`.

AGENTS.md reference: [AGENTS.md:L63-L63](https://github.com/Zsx154855/NEXARA-PRIME/blob/a64769e5742680be83171bb502d49ff4722dc57a/AGENTS.md#L63-L63)

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,158 @@
+"""Read-only, real repository context for provider-bound missions.
+
+The existing ``ToolRuntime`` intentionally restricts file reads to the mission
+workspace.  Repository audits need a different, explicit boundary: an owner
+approved Git repository.  This module is that boundary.  It never writes to
+the repository, never invokes a shell, and produces a canonical hash that is
+persisted with the mission and sent to the provider.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import hashlib
+import json
+import subprocess
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any
+
+from .git_adapter import RealReadOnlyGitDriver
+
+
+class ContextCollectionError(RuntimeError):
+    """The approved repository could not be collected safely."""
+
+
+_SENSITIVE_NAMES = {".env", ".env.local", ".env.production"}
+_SENSITIVE_SUFFIXES = (".pem", ".key", ".p12", ".pfx")
+_TEXT_SUFFIXES = {
+    ".md", ".py", ".pyproject", ".toml", ".yaml", ".yml", ".json",
+    ".txt", ".js", ".ts", ".tsx", ".css", ".sh", ".sql",
+}
+
+
+def _run_git(root: Path, *args: str) -> str:
+    try:
+        result = subprocess.run(
+            ["git", *args],
+            cwd=root,
+            capture_output=True,
+            text=True,
+            check=False,
+            timeout=10,
+        )
+    except (OSError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired) as exc:
+        raise ContextCollectionError(f"git_probe_failed:{type(exc).__name__}") from exc
+    if result.returncode != 0:
+        raise ContextCollectionError(
+            f"git_probe_failed:{' '.join(args)}:{result.stderr.strip()[:240]}"
+        )
+    return result.stdout
+
+
+def _safe_for_provider(relative: str) -> bool:
+    path = Path(relative)
+    if any(part in {".git", "__pycache__", ".venv", "node_modules"} for part in path.parts):
+        return False
+    if path.name in _SENSITIVE_NAMES or path.suffix.lower() in _SENSITIVE_SUFFIXES:
+        return False
+    return True
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class RepositoryContext:
+    repository_root: str
+    branch: str
+    head_sha: str
+    status_porcelain: str
+    files: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]
+    excerpts: dict[str, str]
+    context_hash: str
+
+    def to_provider_context(self, mission_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
+        """Return bounded, secret-filtered context for a real provider call."""
+        return {
+            "mission_id": mission_id,
+            "context_hash": self.context_hash,
+            "repository": {
+                "root": self.repository_root,
+                "branch": self.branch,
+                "head_sha": self.head_sha,
+                "status_porcelain": self.status_porcelain,
+                "dirty": bool(self.status_porcelain),
+            },
+            "files": list(self.files),
+            "excerpts": dict(self.excerpts),
+        }
+
+    def manifest(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
+        """Return metadata suitable for durable evidence without file contents."""
+        return {
+            "repository_root": self.repository_root,
+            "branch": self.branch,
+            "head_sha": self.head_sha,
+            "status_porcelain": self.status_porcelain,
+            "dirty": bool(self.status_porcelain),
+            "file_count": len(self.files),
+            "context_hash": self.context_hash,
+            "files": list(self.files),
+        }
+
+
+class RealRepositoryContext:
+    """Collect deterministic Git and file facts from one approved root."""
+
+    def __init__(self, *, max_files: int = 400, max_excerpt_files: int = 24, max_excerpt_bytes: int = 8_000):
+        self.max_files = max_files
+        self.max_excerpt_files = max_excerpt_files
+        self.max_excerpt_bytes = max_excerpt_bytes
+
+    def collect(self, repository_root: str | Path) -> RepositoryContext:
+        requested = Path(repository_root).expanduser().resolve()
+        if not requested.exists() or not requested.is_dir():
+            raise ContextCollectionError(f"repository_not_found:{requested}")
+        top_level = Path(_run_git(requested, "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel").strip()).resolve()
+        if top_level != requested:
+            raise ContextCollectionError(f"repository_root_mismatch:{top_level}")
+        driver = RealReadOnlyGitDriver(str(requested))
+        branch = driver.current_branch
+        head_sha = driver.get_head_sha()
+        status = driver.status().output.strip()
+        relative_paths = driver.list_files()
+
+        files: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+        excerpts: dict[str, str] = {}
+        for relative in relative_paths[: self.max_files]:
+            path = requested / relative
+            if not _safe_for_provider(relative) or not path.is_file():
+                continue
+            try:
+                data = path.read_bytes()
+            except OSError as exc:
+                raise ContextCollectionError(f"file_read_failed:{relative}") from exc
+            digest = hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
+            files.append({"path": relative, "bytes": len(data), "sha256": digest})
+            if len(excerpts) < self.max_excerpt_files and Path(relative).suffix.lower() in _TEXT_SUFFIXES:
+                excerpts[relative] = data[: self.max_excerpt_bytes].decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Redact repository excerpts before sending them to providers**

For source repos, this copies the first 8KB of every text-like file into `excerpts` after only path-name filtering. A file such as `secrets.yaml` or `settings.py` with `client_secret`/credentials will be included in the model-visible context message because the collector does not run the existing secret scanner or redact the excerpt before storing it, so real-provider missions can leak approved-workspace secrets externally.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Refresh snapshots after mission mutations**

These mutation calls are stored as `MissionSnapshot`, but the backend endpoints return the raw `Mission` model rather than the `inspect_mission()` snapshot. After pause/resume/rollback the UI loses snapshot-only fields such as `title`, `objective`, `provider`, `approval_status`, and `receipt_status` until a full reload, so fetch the mission snapshot after each mutation or make these endpoints return snapshots.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

             trace_id=mission.trace_id, metadata={"from_state": previous, "to_state": target.value},
         )
 
+    def _collect_repository_context(self, mission: Mission) -> RepositoryContext | None:
+        if not mission.spec.source_dir:
+            return None
+        try:
+            source_root = Path(mission.spec.source_dir).resolve()
+            source_root.relative_to(self.settings.workspace_root.resolve())
+        except ValueError:
+            return None
+        try:
+            return self.repository_context.collect(mission.spec.source_dir)
+        except (ContextCollectionError, RuntimeError):
+            return None
+
+    def _execution_context(self, mission: Mission) -> RepositoryContext | None:
+        context = self._collect_repository_context(mission)
+        expected = mission.result.get("context_hash")
+        if expected and (context is None or context.context_hash != expected):
+            raise ValueError("context_hash_mismatch")
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Exclude runtime outputs from context hashes**

When `source_dir` is the repository/workspace root and the report root is inside it, the mission's own report write changes the repository context after planning: `plan_mission()` stores the initial `context_hash`, `_execute_stage()` writes `reports/<mission>/mission-report.md`, and the next `_execution_context()` recomputation sees that untracked report and raises `context_hash_mismatch`, failing an otherwise valid mission during verification. Exclude runtime delivery paths from repository context or compare only immutable source inputs.

AGENTS.md reference: [AGENTS.md:L46-L47](https://github.com/Zsx154855/NEXARA-PRIME/blob/60f5eb625b7d316a09cdd52e937e4e088d7289a3/AGENTS.md#L46-L47)

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Reject symlinks before reading repository files**

If an approved repository contains a tracked or untracked symlink to a file outside the repo, this path check follows the symlink: `path.is_file()` is true for a file target and `read_bytes()` then hashes and may excerpt content from outside the approved source tree into provider context. Resolve each candidate and ensure it remains under `requested`, or skip symlinks entirely before reading.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

+    def verify_receipt_chain(self, mission_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
+        """Verify the complete receipt chain for a mission.
+
+        Every tool invocation must have a linked evidence receipt.
+        Returns a structured report with chain integrity status.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Verify evidence before indexing receipts**

When a mission has a malformed or otherwise untrusted evidence row, this raw `list_records("evidence")` call decodes and then relies on `tool_invocation_id` before the receipt verifier can classify the row as corrupt; malformed JSON will make `/api/receipts` or the independent auditor raise instead of returning an unverifiable receipt-chain report. Build this map from audited envelopes or verify each evidence record before using its payload.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve IP checks for allowlisted domains**

For an allowlisted hostname with no `resolved_ip` supplied, this early return skips DNS resolution and the private/metadata IP checks below, so a DNS-rebound allowlisted domain can be authorized even when it resolves to `169.254.169.254` or an RFC1918 address. Keep resolving and applying the IP safety checks before returning `allowed`, or require callers to pass a resolved IP that is validated here.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Detect V1 NSEC bindings as stale**

This stale-binding regex no longer catches `NEXARA_SOVEREIGN_ENGINEERING_CONSTITUTION_V1`, so a skill or agent entry can keep pointing at the superseded V1 constitution while `detect_nsec_drift.py` still reports no drift. Include V1 in the old-version pattern so the drift gate enforces the current V2.1 canonical binding.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Redact quoted secret keys in repository excerpts**

When a real provider mission includes `.json`/`.yaml` excerpts, labeled secrets using quoted keys such as `"api_key": "prod-secret"` or `"client_secret": "..."` are not matched by this regex, so `RealRepositoryContext` can send those values to the model despite calling `redact_secrets()`. Extend the pattern to handle quoted object keys before the colon.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Validate memory evidence before marking it verified**

For mission-scoped decisions/failures/patches, passing any non-empty `source_evidence_id` persists the memory as `verified` and `canonical` even if that evidence ID does not exist, belongs to another mission, or fails `EvidenceStore.verify()`. Validate the evidence binding before setting these flags so consumers do not read forged memory as verified.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Classify authorization denials through the receipt path**

Only exceptions caught in this block receive `failure_code`/`reason_code` and a tool receipt; approval failures raised earlier (`missing_approval_id` or invalid approval) still leave no failed `ToolInvocation`, so receipt-chain/auditor views report no classified failure for those denied R2 writes. Move approval validation into the same fail-closed invocation path or persist a failed receipt before re-raising.
